coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Titles in the record include "Senior Analyst and Partner", "Partner and Senior Research Analyst", and "Senior Research Analyst" across calls (e.g. 2022-Q1, 2023-Q1, 2025-Q1). Management addresses him by name and he appears regularly in the middle-early part of Q&A, consistent with a senior, repeat coverage role.
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Primarily cruise/leisure/travel with frequent cross-reference to lodging and broader travel (e.g. Marriott/Airbnb comparisons in 2022-Q1) and occasional discussion of luxury/ultra-luxury and OTA distribution (2023-Q3, 2024-Q1).
rhetorical_signature: Direct, composed questioning that strings 2–3 sub-questions into one turn (see many examples such as 2023-Q1 and 2023-Q3).
Polite, conversational tone with short closers like "Great. Thanks" or "Thanks" appearing repeatedly (examples: 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2023-Q1).
Frequently asks for decompositions ("like for like, versus new hardware versus CocoCay lift" in 2023-Q1) and uses agent/guest anecdotes to illustrate points (plane anecdote in 2022-Q3).

reasoning_style.justification: Questions routinely combine quantitative probes (yields, APD, % changes, passenger counts, CapEx) with strategic framing (value gap to land, product positioning, loyalty). Examples: decomposition of net per diems into like‑for‑like/new hardware/CocoCay (2023-Q1) and ROIC discussion (2023-Q1).
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Multi-part questions are the norm; many turns contain two or more explicit asks (≈ 0.60 of turns contain ≥2 question clauses by inspection of sample turns such as 2023-Q1, 2023-Q3, 2024-Q2). He also takes follow-up turns within the same call for clarification or a different subtopic (consecutive turns in 2022-Q1 and 2022-Q2).
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He consistently asks for magnitudes and decompositions: % changes ("double digits", "mid-single-digits" — 2023-Q1/2023-Q3), passenger counts ("2.5 million", "3.5 million" — 2023-Q2), $ and average pricing anecdotes (e.g., "$75,000 a week" for a family townhouse on Icon cited by mgmt in 2022-Q4). He rewards explicit guidance, like booked-rate trajectory, and asks for mechanism (mix vs like‑for‑like).
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: He frequently anchors against the 2019 baseline and management guidance (e.g., asks about returns to 2019 levels in 2022-Q1 and references 2019 repeatedly across 2022–2024). He also anchors to management's just‑stated guides or phrases in the call (e.g., "the guide for 3Q" in 2022-Q2 and "net per diems ... out of high singles" in 2023-Q1) and occasionally references peers/land-based comparables (Marriott/Airbnb in 2022-Q1).

core_topics.what_they_press_on: Break down where yield upside comes from (like‑for‑like vs new hardware vs destination lift) and trajectory into out years; repeatedly asks for decomposition and whether gains are sustainable.
How new ships and private destinations are altering demand, ticket lift, onboard mix, and whether those effects persist multi‑year.
Sustainability of elevated onboard per passenger cruise day and whether UX/technology (pre‑cruise booking) or guest mix is driving higher onboard take rates.
Longer‑term non‑fuel unit cost trajectory, fuel mix (MGO vs IFO), consumption per ALBD, and the implications of dry docks/maintenance on NCC and modeling.
How industry order book and near‑term delivery cadence affect pricing power and whether a multi‑year muted supply helps sustain above‑historical yield growth.
Cross‑brand reciprocity, share of wallet, repeat booking behavior and whether better loyalty can drive frequency and unit economics.
Balance between returning capital (dividend/repurchases) and funding growth (ships, destinations); questions on multi‑year CapEx cadence and opportunistic buybacks.

recurring_concerns.blind_spots: Relatively little sustained questioning on environmental/ESG specifics (e.g., emissions targets, detailed fuel‑hedge strategy) after an early 2022 fuel question; peers sometimes press fleet emissions/green fuel plans more directly, but Vince has not returned consistently to those specifics. He also rarely parses crew/operational labor detail beyond unit‑cost commentary.
recurring_concerns.stance_drift: Early (2022-Q1/2022-Q2) his focus was on pricing vs land‑based comparables and unit costs; by 2023 (2023-Q1/2023-Q3) the emphasis shifted toward yield decomposition and how new hardware/destinations are driving outsized price gains; by 2024–2025 (2024-Q2/2025-Q2) he added more strategic questions on loyalty, river cruising and multi‑year CapEx/share repurchase tradeoffs.

queue_behavior.typical_position: #3–#8 of the active questioners on most calls; across the 15 calls listed he appears middle‑early (examples: #3 in 2022-Q3 and 2024-Q2, #4 in many calls such as 2022-Q3/2022-Q4/2024-Q3), and he was #10 on 2025-Q3 (one late instance).
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When early to speak (no topics taken yet) he typically opens on pricing/booking dynamics or occupancy (see 2022-Q3 where as #3 he asked about occupancy progress and drivers; 2023-Q1 as #4 he probed pricing decomposition).

evidence.quote: how much of that's like for like, versus new hardware
how would you slice up that 13%-ish growth
have you ever thought about getting into river cruising
conviction level of getting to 35-40 ships
can you comment on what you expect bookings growth to look like

explicit_peer_references.note: No explicit "following up on X's question" phrasing found in the provided history; he more commonly reacts to management lines rather than explicitly citing other analysts.

examples.quote: guide for 3Q came in a little bit higher
net per diems for this year to be out of high singles

management_line_anchoring.note: He frequently opens by referencing the guide, book position, or mgmt language on APD/yield and then asks for decomposition or implications.

cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: He often takes a consecutive/second turn within the same call when allowed, typically to drill into a different subtopic (e.g., pricing then costs in 2022-Q1 where he had back‑to‑back turns; 2022-Q2 shows consecutive fuel and cost follow-ups). When he gets a second turn it is used for clarification or to ask a related but distinct modeling question rather than an unrelated new topic.
